results.title: P.G-H: a modern odyssey
results.creator_credit: I. James Wilson, John Tristram, Film Finance Corporation Australia and Juniper Films
results.brief_description: The life and times of the Australian born composer, Peggy Glanville-Hicks, also known as PG-H, who lived and worked in America with world renowned writers, musicians and choreographers. She became a distinguished music critic for the Hreald Tribune. Her compositions include concertos, sonatas and works for the theatre.
results.creator_credit_for_label: I. James Wilson, John Tristram, Film Finance Corporation Australia and Juniper Films

results.title: Oz: A Rock 'n' Roll Road Movie
results.creator_credit: Chris Lofven, Lyne Helms and Count Features
results.brief_description: An Australian rock movie fable. Dorothy’s teenage quest in the Australia of the 70’s is a modern parable set to Ross Wilson’s music. With three weird friends who alternate between being pals and making passes, Dorothy sets out to find the answer to fame and fortune’s riddle at the hands of the wonderful wizard.

results.title: Oyster man
results.creator_credit: Jean Palardy, Julian Biggs, Michael Spencer and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: The story of oyster farming in the Gulf of St. Lawrence, showing the various stages of oyster cultivation: the sampling of minute larvae by Department of Fisheries biologists, the staking of oyster beds by farmers, the nurturing of spat, and underwater scenes showing the dragging of the sea bed with cotton mops to ensnare starfish which prey upon the oyster. Final sequences show the grading and shipping of oysters and their arrival at the sea food restaurant or family dinner table, where they delight the eyes and palates of oyster lovers.

results.title: Decoder
results.brief_description: The behaviour of the people in the Western World is being altered by an aural weapon devised to subconsciously affect those exposed to it. This artificial product, created by scientists, musicians and marketing experts is called “Musak” or “functional music”. The authors of this film carried out intensive research before making “Decoder” as the function of “musak” is to stimulate or soothe, enhancing wellbeing by constructing a particular tonal sequence. “Musak” by its very nature has an undoubted political significance. This production blends reality and fiction: surreal, metaphorical imagery interwoven with music.

results.title: Decisions, Decisions! (TEFC)
results.brief_description: Illustrates problems managers can encounter when failing to approach decision making and implementation logically. Demonstrates the importance of utilising the knowledge and views of the team when deciding on matters affecting them. Famous decision makers from the past, Brutus, Queen Elizabeth I, Churchill and Montgomery, act as a review panel and confront an incompetent manager with his mistakes. Features John Cleese and Prunella Scales. FOR USE ONLY IN A DOMESTIC SITUATION OR FOR EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY.

results.title: Decisions, decisions (NP)
results.brief_description: Illustrates some of the problems an office manager can come up against if he fails to approach decision making and implementation in a logical manner. Demonstrates how important it is for managers to use the knowledge and views of their team when deciding on matters that affect the team. Famous decision makers from the past, Brutus, Queen Elizabeth I, Churchill, Montgomery act as a review panel and confront an incompetent manager with his mistakes. Features John Cleese. FOR USE ONLY IN A DOMESTIC SITUATION OR FOR EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY.

results.title: Overture
results.creator_credit: United Nations. Dept. of Public Information
results.brief_description: Beethoven’s “Egmont Overture”, played by the Vienna Philharmonic Orchestra, conducted by Carl Schuricht, is a musical background for scenes symbolising the work of the United Nations in combating disease, ignorance, war, devastation, starvation and misery.

results.title: Overspill
results.creator_credit: Mort Ransen, John Kemeny, Tom Daly, National Film Board of Canada and Svenska filminstitutet
results.brief_description: A film about Norrbotten, in northernmost Sweden, where industrial and agricultural patterns have changed and people are being relocated in industrial towns far from the life they have loved. In a series of interviews we meet people who have known the dignity of independence and who have suddenly become welfare cases. The film asks: Are their lives being improved? How can we reconcile human values and economic needs?
